diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Monocular central visual loss fits optic neuritis.
Acute visual change can also be ischaemic; cannot discriminate remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Painful eye movements with colour desaturation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Painful eye movements plus red/colour desaturation are characteristic of optic neuritis.
A second CNS sensory symptom is consistent with a demyelinating syndrome.
Facial sensory change can be ischaemic; keeps stroke in play.

symptoms.symptom_name: No limb weakness, dysarthria, facial droop or ataxia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of classic anterior-circulation signs lowers, but does not exclude, stroke (posterior circulation).

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent brain imaging (CT/CTA then MRI) via ED/acute neurology
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Needed urgently to exclude stroke and, later, characterise demyelination — cannot be arranged remotely as outpatient in this acute window.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: MRI brain and orbits with contrast
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Characterises optic neuritis/demyelination once stroke excluded; arranged through the acute assessment, not deferred to routine outpatient in 48 h.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Formal ophthalmology/visual acuity and RAPD assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms optic neuritis; requires in-person examination.
